# RQ-0811 - Plan

## Task Goal
Refactor `ralph task --help` from a single flat list of 20+ subcommands into workflow-oriented sections so new users can find the right action by intent (create/build, lifecycle, edit, relationships, batch/template) and see common journey examples (create task, start task, complete task, split task) without guessing adjacent command names.

## Why
The current task CLI help exposes subcommands in a linear order and omits higher-level grouping, which causes cognitive friction (`field` vs `edit`, `status` vs `done`) and weakens onboarding. Grouping command metadata and adding journey-oriented help output directly addresses discoverability with minimal UX-only behavior changes.

## Baseline Observed
- `TaskCommand` in `crates/ralph/src/cli/task/args.rs` currently defines all variants without `help_heading` grouping.
- Root `TaskArgs` help text is one large mixed example list.
- `help_output_contract_test.rs` only asserts that `task --help` contains `ralph task` and `build`; there are no assertions for grouped sections.
- `docs/cli.md` task section enumerates commands as one flat list and command-by-command sections.

## Plan (Phase 2)

### 1) Group `TaskCommand` variants by workflow intent in `crates/ralph/src/cli/task/args.rs`

Add `help_heading` to each `TaskCommand` variant using the exact headings below to force intentional sections in clap output:
- `Create and build`
- `Lifecycle`
- `Edit`
- `Relationships`
- `Batch and templates`

Suggested variant mapping:
- Create and build: `Build`, `Refactor`, `BuildRefactor`, `Template`
- Lifecycle: `Show`, `Ready`, `Status`, `Done`, `Reject`, `Start`, `Schedule`
- Edit: `Field`, `Edit`, `Update`
- Relationships: `Clone`, `Split`, `Relate`, `Blocks`, `MarkDuplicate`, `Children`, `Parent`
- Batch and templates: `Batch`

Snippet:
```rust
#[derive(Subcommand)]
pub enum TaskCommand {
    /// Build a new task from a natural language request.
    #[command(help_heading = "Create and build")]
    Build(TaskBuildArgs),

    /// Automatically create refactoring tasks for large files.
    #[command(alias = "ref", help_heading = "Create and build")]
    Refactor(TaskBuildRefactorArgs),

    /// Automatically create refactoring tasks for large files (alternative to 'refactor').
    #[command(name = "build-refactor", help_heading = "Create and build")]
    BuildRefactor(TaskBuildRefactorArgs),

    /// Show a task by ID (queue + done).
    #[command(help_heading = "Lifecycle")]
    Show(TaskShowArgs),

    /// ... etc
    #[command(help_heading = "Edit")]
    Edit(TaskEditArgs),
}
```

Also preserve all existing command names/aliases so no runtime behavior changes beyond presentation.

### 2) Add explicit ‚Äúcommon journeys‚Äù to `TaskArgs` root after-long-help

Replace the current flat example blob in `TaskArgs` with grouped examples and a dedicated section at help depth for first-run journeys.

Snippet:
```rust
#[derive(Args)]
#[command(
    about = "Create and manage tasks",
    subcommand_required = false,
    after_long_help = "Common journeys:
 - Create a task:
   ralph task \"Refactor queue parsing\"
   ralph task build-refactor
 - Start work on a task:
   ralph task ready RQ-0001
   ralph task start RQ-0001
 - Complete a task:
   ralph task status done RQ-0001
   ralph task done --note \"Build checks pass\" RQ-0001
 - Split a task:
   ralph task split RQ-0001
   ralph task split --number 3 RQ-0001\n\nCommand intent sections:
Create and build: task, build, refactor, build-refactor, template
Lifecycle: show, ready, status, done, reject, start, schedule
Edit: field, edit, update
Relationships: clone, split, relate, blocks, mark-duplicate, children, parent
Batch and templates: batch"
)]
pub struct TaskArgs { ... }
```

### 3) Update help-contract tests in `crates/ralph/tests/help_output_contract_test.rs`

Add/replace task-help assertions so this is a contract, not a soft target:
- Add a new test that checks section headings appear exactly once and stable.
- Add a new test that checks each ‚ÄúCommon journeys‚Äù line is present.
- Keep existing checks for `build` visibility and add explicit `template`/`done`/`split` references from journey examples.

Snippet:
```rust
#[test]
fn task_help_shows_group_headings() {
    let (status, stdout, stderr) = run(&["task", "--help"]);
    assert!(status.success(), "...");

    let combined = format!("{stdout}\n{stderr}");
    assert!(combined.contains("Create and build"));
    assert!(combined.contains("Lifecycle"));
    assert!(combined.contains("Edit"));
    assert!(combined.contains("Relationships"));
    assert!(combined.contains("Batch and templates"));
}

#[test]
fn task_help_shows_common_journeys() {
    let (status, stdout, stderr) = run(&["task", "--help"]);
    assert!(status.success(), "...");

    let combined = format!("{stdout}\n{stderr}");
    assert!(combined.contains("Common journeys:"));
    assert!(combined.contains("Create a task:"));
    assert!(combined.contains("Start work on a task:"));
    assert!(combined.contains("Complete a task:"));
    assert!(combined.contains("Split a task:"));
}
```

### 4) Refresh task docs in `docs/cli.md`

Rework the `## `ralph task` section from flat subcommand bullets into grouped workflow sections and add a ‚ÄúCommon journeys‚Äù subsection with the same four journey examples.

Suggested replacement structure:
- Intro for `ralph task`
- **Common journeys** (Create / Start / Complete / Split)
- **Create and build** command bullets
- **Lifecycle** command bullets
- **Edit and update** command bullets
- **Relationships** command bullets
- **Batch and templates** command bullets

Keep existing per-command sections (e.g., `### ralph task edit`, `### ralph task batch`, etc.) for detailed arguments and ensure each remains accurate.

### 5) Optional stabilization adjustments

If headings appear out-of-order due Clap behavior, use explicit variant ordering and/or consistent heading capitalization to enforce deterministic output. Avoid changing any non-PH1 scope.

## Validation checklist for execution handoff
- Run `cargo run -p ralph -- task --help` and confirm all five sections render in the expected order.
- Confirm ‚ÄúCommon journeys‚Äù text is visible and includes all four lifecycle paths.
- Run `cargo test -p ralph` or at minimum the help contract tests; ensure new/updated task-help tests pass.
- Keep command compatibility: all existing subcommand names and aliases remain unchanged.

## If assumptions are invalid
- If `TaskCommand` or `TaskArgs` is refactored differently in this branch, apply the same grouping intent and wording in the current equivalent structs rather than forcing strict one-for-one variant names.
